summary: Basic mobile FPS weapon feel now includes faster movement, body-only resolved player collision, wall-shot detection, full-surface zoom-safe touch input, visual weapon wall clearance, wider structural entries, splitter-post clearance, shared shot-effect pose alignment, smoothed hold-fire ADS-style zoom/centering, distinct per-weapon shot colors, larger centered guns, reload HUD timing, and steel foundation room surfaces. Enemy offense now includes line-of-sight projectiles and lower cooldown-gated contact damage against the 25 HP player baseline.
observations: The right-side fire button calls the weapon system without stealing aim drags elsewhere on the look zone.
SPARK, BORE, and VAULT use different cadence and recoil values.
Ammo and reload state are exposed in the HUD and debug API.
Player movement speed is now 5.078125 units per second.
Last shot debug state reported a wall hit at tile [22, 1] from spawn-facing fire smoke.
Player movement uses resolveLevelCollision with PLAYER_COLLISION_RADIUS and no longer treats the weapon footprint as a hard blocker.
Weapon-only footprint overlap remains detectable through collidesWithWeaponFootprint for debug and wall-avoidance reasoning.
WeaponSystem exposes wallAvoidance in the weapon debug snapshot.
Right-shifted viewmodels reduce overlap with the bottom-center weapon tray.
Structural wall-band entries now require 5 open tiles so right-shifted weapon presentation has readable room in door-like breaks.
Structural entry splitter posts now require at least 3 open tiles per side and 6 combined open tiles.
The bottom row 39 entries near world x -10 and x 8.2 were widened to keep 3-tile branches around the adjacent divider posts.
Shot feedback uses a shared weapon view pose so tracer start follows active weapon offset, recoil, and wall retraction.
Accidental double-tap, pinch, WebKit gesture, and ctrl-wheel zoom paths are guarded while keeping move/look pointer ownership stable.
Foundation walls now render at 3.84 units tall with a 3.92-unit roof.
Starter weapon view scales were increased again by about 22% and each weapon now has a right-handed hip pose plus a scope-like centered hold-fire aim pose.
Holding the fire button immediately fires, continues firing by cadence, zooms the camera from 70 to 62 degrees, and blends the gun toward a centered aim pose.
Hold-fire aim now uses eased damping before applying FOV and viewmodel pose so the zoom-in/zoom-out transition feels less jerky.
The fire button can drive reduced-sensitivity look while held so the right thumb can aim and fire together.
The fire button is represented by a reticle icon; weapon switching uses the smaller right-thumb gun-icon cycle button beside it.
SPARK, BORE, and VAULT now have distinct placeholder shot colors and effect sizes for readability while final external VFX assets are pending.
Weapon debug state now includes isFireHeld, aimBlend, and cameraFovDegrees.
The foundation level now uses steel-toned Kenney CC0 prototype textures for floor, walls, and roof.
A full-level roof plane sits above the wall height.
Tracking enemies fire visible energy-orb projectiles when the player is in range and line of sight.
Enemy projectiles damage the shared player Health instance, so the HP bar updates from enemy hits.
Enemy movement speed increased 25% for all current behaviors: Mushnub 1.6, slime 1.775, and Goleling 1.275 units per second.
Enemies now clamp projectile cooldown to 0.08 seconds when first entering tracking, then return to normal cooldown cadence.
Enemy projectiles were enlarged and depth-tested off so the temporary additive orb reads more clearly in first-person view.
Player max health is now 25 for the current MVP combat tuning baseline.
Enemies now deal lower contact damage when the player overlaps their body: Mushnub 3, slime 3, and Goleling 4.
Contact damage is cooldown-gated per enemy so collision does not damage every frame.
Weapon debug state now includes reloadRemainingMs and reloadProgress for normal HUD reload feedback.
decisions: Keep one fire button for now.
Use Space and number keys only as desktop smoke-test helpers.
Keep procedural audio until ElevenLabs assets are intentionally generated.
Keep shot wall feedback lightweight until proper tracer and impact assets are sourced.
Use body-only movement collision and viewmodel retraction for rotation-near-wall cases.
Keep structural wall-band entries wider than the ordinary 3-tile traversal lane minimum.
Do not center continuing wall posts inside structural entries unless both resulting branches meet the splitter clearance rule.
Do not allow later non-UI touches to overwrite active move/look pointer IDs.
Treat the fire button as the current mobile ADS-plus-trigger hold for MVP.
Use eased aim blend for camera FOV and weapon pose rather than a linear step.
Keep fire-button drag aiming slower than the main look zone.
Keep per-weapon aim poses in the manifest because starter gun silhouettes differ.
Use low-damage MVP enemy projectiles before adding final external projectile VFX assets.
Block enemy projectile firing through tile walls and retry after a short cooldown.
Keep first-shot tracking response fast, then use normal projectile cooldown cadence for follow-up shots.
Keep contact damage below projectile damage and cadence-gated so accidental collision is a warning, not an instant death spiral.
caughtIssues: Fast repeated fire and full-canvas look taps needed explicit double-tap zoom prevention.
Weapon-only movement blocking caused unrecoverable stuck states when the player turned near entries.
Tap-only fire did not provide enough FPS feel; the fire action needed continuous cadence, aim zoom, and centered weapon framing.
The first hold-fire zoom response felt too abrupt; future changes should keep the eased damping behavior or replace it with a better measured transition.
Enemies previously had movement and health but no offensive pressure against the player.
Spawn-staggered projectile cooldowns made enemies feel like they were not shooting when first acquired.
Player-enemy overlap previously had no consequence, so running through enemies could bypass room pressure.
risks: Without hit detection or enemy response, weapon feel can only be judged on cadence, recoil, audio, and UI responsiveness.
Weapon presentation can still visually crowd tight turns if future maps shrink below the 3-tile lane rule.
Physical mobile Safari still needs direct pinch/double-tap verification.
Door-like entries below 5 tiles can feel narrower than their symbol-map width with a right-shifted weapon.
A 5-tile entry can still feel like two 2-tile branches when a continuing divider post sits just inside the opening.
Physical devices should verify that reduced-sensitivity fire-button look remains controllable under thumb pressure.
